summary: date-format-helper2 postinstall exfiltrates Coze tokens,.npmrc and project secrets to attacker-controlled requestrepo.com
details: Package advertises itself as 'A simple date formatting utility for React projects' but ships a postinstall.js that runs at npm install time and performs targeted credential theft against Coze (ByteDance) workload sandboxes. On install the script (1) enumerates process.env for any keys containing 'COZE'/'coze' and POSTs them to http://5l8e0e95.requestrepo.com over plain HTTP, (2) shells out to read /root/.npmrc (capturing the installer's npm registry auth token) and /source/web_supervisord.conf, (3) walks /workspace for.env,.env.*, config.json and credentials.json files and cats their contents, transmitting each capture to the same attacker endpoint, and (4) calls Coze APIs with the stolen tokens and forwards the responses back to the attacker. The index.js date-formatting code is a cover story; the install-time payload is unrelated to the advertised functionality and harms any installer. The destination 5l8e0e95.requestrepo.com is a requestrepo.com canary subdomain, a service commonly abused for out-of-band data capture.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
